justification: Both extractions are nearly identical in quality across all four dimensions. Both accurately extract skills demonstrated in the resume without fabricating Spark, Kafka, or ETL experience. Both are well-calibrated in skill count (similar categories and counts). Neither output includes explicit project_type taxonomy classifications, so both score neutral on project_classification. Both capture the concrete metrics faithfully: 52% latency reduction (270s to 130s), 98% time reduction, 83% balanced accuracy, 5 hrs/week saved, 1,000+ orders, 95%+ accuracy. The minor differences — B retains 'JWKS' detail in Internship Matcher and 'B2B' in Burnt bullet, A uses 'codebase complexity' vs B's 'code complexity' — are trivial and do not meaningfully differentiate quality. All four dimension scores are within 0 points of each other, satisfying the tie condition.
